title: Snowdenia mutica

This family, commonly known as the ice plant or stone plant family, consists of succulent plants native to southern Africa. Snowdenia mutica is a genus of succulent plants that belongs to the tribe Ruschieae. The plant's classification is based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics, as well as its genetic relationships with other species in the family. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification and relationships of Snowdenia mutica.
